reasoning: Initial observation shows d1=10, d2=20, d3=30 and total_distance=60. The sum of the segments (10+20+30) equals the total distance (60). To confirm this hypothesis, I will increase d1 by 5 units and observe if total_distance increases by exactly 5 units.

In Step 0, increasing d1 from 10 to 20 caused total_distance to increase from 60 to 70, a difference of exactly 10. This suggests total_distance is the sum of d1, d2, and d3. To further confirm this hypothesis, I will increase d2 by 5 units and check if total_distance increases by exactly 5 units.

In Step 0, increasing d1 from 10 to 20 caused total_distance to increase from 60 to 70 (delta +10). In Step 1, increasing d2 from 20 to 30 caused total_distance to increase from 70 to 80 (delta +10). The current state shows d1=20, d2=30, d3=30, and total_distance=80. The sum of the segments (20+30+30) equals the total distance (80). This strongly suggests the relationship is total_distance = d1 + d2 + d3. To be absolutely certain, I will perform one more test by decreasing d3 and verifying the total_distance decreases by the same amount.

In Step 0, increasing d1 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. In Step 1, increasing d2 by 10 increased total_distance by 10. In Step 2, decreasing d3 by 10 decreased total_distance by 10. In all cases, the change in total_distance equals the change in the modified variable, while other variables remained constant. The current state (d1=20, d2=30, d3=20) yields a sum of 70, which matches the observed total_distance. This confirms the relationship is additive.
